target.name: Form Energy
target.founder: Mateo Jaramillo, Yet-Ming Chiang, Ted Wiley, William Woodford, Marco Ferrara

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $3.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $3.3B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: TAE Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series G), post-money $6B

data.narrative:
Form Energy, a Series G climate and energy technology company founded by Mateo Jaramillo and co-founders, has raised $690M+ to date at a last post-money valuation of $3.5B. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 comparable companies—1 public (LNZA) and 8 private—operating in the same sector and stage, including TAE Technologies, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, applying a typical 1.25x multiplier to the Series G post-money, implies a valuation of $4.4B. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—TAE Technologies ($6.0B), Helion ($5.4B), and Sila Nanotechnologies ($3.3B)—yields a median valuation of $5.4B with a range of $3.3B to $6.0B.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $3.7B to $6.2B, with a median implication of $4.9B. The $1.6B spread reflects the inherent uncertainty in late-stage climate-tech valuations, where peer comparables span different funding rounds and technology maturity profiles. Key variables affecting valuation include technology validation progress, capital intensity of commercialization, and macroeconomic conditions affecting energy infrastructure funding. The current $3.5B valuation sits at the lower quartile of this range, suggesting upside potential conditional on demonstrated technical and commercial progress.
